text: Tell us about yourself and your academic background.
Why are you interested in this specific program and university?
Describe your research interests and how they align with the work being done in this department.
Which faculty members are you interested in working with, and why?
Walk me through your most significant research experience. What was your specific contribution?
What research question do you want to explore during your graduate studies?
Where do you see yourself in five to ten years after completing this program?
How has your academic and professional experience prepared you for graduate study?
Describe a time when an experiment or project did not go as planned. How did you adapt?
What is a paper or study in your field that has significantly influenced your thinking?
How would you handle disagreements with your advisor about the direction of your research?
What are the most important methodological skills you bring to this program?
Tell us about a time you had to explain a complex concept to someone outside your field.

material: Graduate programs often require balancing coursework, research, teaching responsibilities, and personal well-being, particularly over a 4-6 year PhD timeline.
text: How do you plan to manage these competing demands, and what strategies have you used in the past to maintain productivity during challenging periods?
What other programs have you applied to, and what makes this one your preferred choice?
Describe your experience with [specific methodology relevant to the program: statistical analysis, lab techniques, fieldwork, etc.].
What do you consider to be the biggest open question or challenge in your field right now?

material: You are conducting research and discover that your preliminary results contradict the hypothesis proposed in your advisor's recent publication.
text: How would you approach this situation?
What experience do you have with teaching or mentoring? How has it influenced your approach to your own learning?
How would you contribute to the intellectual community of this department beyond your own research?
Is there anything else about your candidacy that you would like us to know?
